detail: Question asks for predicted y at x=30, which is T7 (predict y) logic, not T8 (residual = actual minus predicted). No actual data point given; only the line and an interpolated x. Answer math (≈6.3) is correct, but type is mistagged.

Question asks for predicted y at x=6 using the line of best fit — this is T7 logic (predict y at given x), not T8 (residual = actual minus predicted). No actual data point is given. Answer math (6.33→6) is correct, but type is mistagged.
Tagged A6.T12 (exponential curve parameter), but clone asks for slope of a linear line of best fit through (0,15) and (12,135). This is A3.T5 (compute slope), not A6 (exponential parameter).
Tagged A3.T6 (interpret slope in context) but clone asks for a predicted y-value at x=6 using the line, which is A4.T7 (predict y at given x). Not slope interpretation.
Tagged A3.T5 (compute slope of best-fit line) but clone asks for predicted y at x=900, which is A4.T7 (predict y at given x). Answer is correct (9), but aspect tag is wrong.
Clone reads a value directly from a data table with no line of best fit or prediction model. This is A1 (read raw values), not A4 (predict using model).
T10 requires classifying among {linear/exponential × increasing/decreasing} model families. Clone instead asks to describe the association direction; MCQ options do not span the 2x2 model grid.

T11 must_preserve requires a clearly nonlinear (typically exponential) scatterplot with curve options. Clone shows linear data with linear-vs-constant-vs-quadratic options; correct answer is 'increasing linear', so it fits A5.T10 not T11.
Clone reads weight directly from a listed data point (5, 50). No line of best fit or prediction model is used. This is A1 reading raw values, not A4 model-based prediction.
Clone looks up 50 mph row directly in the table (180 ft). No line of best fit or model prediction is applied; this fits A1, not A4.
T2 must_preserve requires a contextualized x-y graph (not a generic scatterplot or table). Clone uses a table of values, not a graph; structure better matches A1.T1 medium (interval-change reading).
T2 must_preserve requires a contextualized x-y graph. Clone presents only a verbal/bulleted description with no plotted graph, violating the structural requirement.
Tagged A1.T2 (qualitative graph reading, no equation/numeric prediction). Stem asks for predicted density via slope computation at x=55%—this is A4.T7 prediction, not A1 reading.

Tagged T2 (contextual graph, qualitative property). Stem uses a table (not graph) and asks for a numeric difference (200-90=110). Closer to T1 (compare values) but T2 must_preserve specifies 'contextualized x-y graph, not table' and 'no equation/numeric computation.'

Medium T5 lever requires slope between 0 and 1 (or -1 and 0) so sign+magnitude both diagnostic, and decimal/non-integer reads. Clone uses integer slope = 3 from clean integer coordinates; this matches Easy lever, not Medium.

T6.hard requires slope scaled by non-unit interval (per 5/10 units) OR slope estimated from figure first. Clone asks for plain per-unit increase = slope from two given coords, which is just T5 slope computation, not T6 interpretation.

Clone asks about greatest increase between consecutive x-values requiring computing four differences (+15,-4,-2,+2). Per template, this matches T1.medium ('interval requiring two reads plus subtraction'), not easy ('no calculation required, extreme value visually obvious').
